title: Migrant Workers and Other Overseas Filipinos Resource Center
content:
Section 17 establishes a Migrant Workers and other Overseas Filipinos Resource Center in countries with large concentrations of OFWs, under the administrative jurisdiction of the Philippine Embassy or Consulate. When established outside embassy or consulate premises, the Department of Foreign Affairs shall seek recognition from the receiving government.

Section 18 outlines the services provided by the Resource Center, including counseling and legal services; welfare assistance such as medical and hospitalization procurement; information and advisory programs for social integration; registration of irregular/undocumented workers; implementation of DOLE and OWWA programs; human resource development; gender-sensitive programs; orientation for returning workers; monitoring of migrant workers' situations; ensuring fair application of labor and social welfare laws in receiving countries; and conciliation of employer-employee disputes per the Rule.

metadata.source_file: Omnibus IRR of RA 8042 as amended by RA 10022.pdf
